§ 01Your Inputs

These are the values you provided. The calculator uses these to estimate your net proceeds under different scenarios.

§ 02Scenario A: ESOP Sale (with §1042)

If you sell to an ESOP and make the §1042 election, reinvesting 100% of proceeds in Qualified Replacement Securities:

ItemAmount
Total purchase price{{indicativeFMV}}
Day 1 cash (SBA proceeds){{day1Cash}}
Seller note (deferred){{sellerNoteAmount}}
Seller note interest ({{sellerNoteRate}}){{sellerNoteInterest}}
Total consideration{{totalConsideration}}
Federal tax at closing (§1042)$0
State tax at closing{{stateTaxESOP}}
Transaction costs (est.){{transactionCosts}}
Net at closing (Day 1){{netAtClosingESOP}}
Deferred gain (taxed when QRS sold){{capitalGain}}
§ 03Scenario B: PE Sale (for comparison)

If you sell to a private equity firm at the same price (illustrative only — PE offers vary):

ItemAmount
Total purchase price{{indicativeFMV}}
Less: PE transaction costs({{peTransactionCosts}})
Less: federal LTCG (20%)({{federalLTCG}})
Less: NIIT (3.8%)({{niit}})
Less: state income tax({{stateTaxPE}})
Net after-tax proceeds{{netProceedsPE}}
§ 04Side-by-Side
MetricESOP + §1042PE Sale
Tax at closing{{totalTaxESOP}}{{totalTaxPE}}
Net at closing{{netAtClosingESOP}}{{netProceedsPE}}
Deferred gain{{capitalGain}}$0
Tax savings at closing{{taxSavings}}
Important: This calculator provides indicative estimates only. Actual proceeds depend on the independent FMV appraisal, final deal structure, and your specific tax situation. Your CPA should verify all tax calculations. PE offers vary widely and may include earnouts, equity rollovers, or holdbacks not modeled here.
§ 05Seller Note Payout Schedule

If the seller note is part of the structure, here is the estimated payout:

{{sellerNoteAmount}}
{{sellerNoteRate}}
{{sellerNoteTerm}}
{{annualPayment}}
{{totalPayments}}
{{totalInterest}}
Note: The seller note is subordinated to the SBA loan. Payments begin after the SBA loan is current. During subordination, interest accrues but is not paid. Once subordination is released, principal and interest payments begin.
§ 06Next Steps

These numbers are indicative. To get a precise picture:

1.Get a formal appraisal. The independent appraiser will determine the actual FMV based on QofE analysis.
2.Consult your CPA. Have your CPA verify the tax calculations using your actual basis, filing status, and state.
3.Review the capital stack. The final Day 1 cash depends on the SBA loan terms, which are confirmed after lender underwriting.
